title: Wishmax Design System — Adopt, Materialize & Enforce

userStories.title: Materialize design tokens (web + RN sources)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want DESIGN.md's tokens as importable files in design/ so the showcase and downstream app PRDs reference tokens instead of literals.
acceptanceCriteria: design/tokens.css exists as a Tailwind v4 @theme block with every color, gradient, type-scale, weight, spacing, radius, shadow, layout, and surface token from DESIGN.md.

design/theme.ts mirrors the same values, adapting CSS-only constructs for RN (gradient stops for expo-linear-gradient, shadows as shadow/elevation props, full-pill 960px radius as a large finite number).
design/README.md states DESIGN.md is the source of truth, documents how to keep the two token files in sync, and lists intentional RN adaptations.
Token values match DESIGN.md for every platform-portable value (spot-check colors, type scale, radii, spacing).
theme.ts typechecks standalone via a minimal tsconfig in design/.
Typecheck passes
verification: Run tsc against design/tsconfig (theme.ts) — no errors.
Diff token values in tokens.css and theme.ts against the DESIGN.md token tables.
userStories.notes: Materialized design/tokens.css (Tailwind v4 @theme), design/theme.ts (RN mirror, typed `as const`), design/README.md (sync rules + RN adaptations), design/tsconfig.json + package.json. theme.ts typechecks clean via tsc -p design/tsconfig.json.

userStories.title: Scaffold the design-system showcase harness
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a minimal design-system-owned web harness wired to the tokens so components have a runtime to build and verify in (not the PRD-02 marketing app or PRD-03 Expo app).
acceptanceCriteria: A minimal web app or Storybook lives under design/ (e.g. design/showcase) — explicitly not a product app.
Tailwind v4 is configured to consume design/tokens.css; a token swatch/sample page renders entirely from tokens (no hardcoded values).
A dev command serves the harness and a build command succeeds; lint passes.
Dev/build commands documented in design/README.md and the AGENTS.md Unknown Stack section.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill
verification: Start the dev server, load the page, confirm swatches reflect the token values.
Run the build, typecheck, and lint commands.
userStories.notes: Scaffolded design/showcase/ (Vite 6 + React 19 + TS + Tailwind v4 via @tailwindcss/vite). src/index.css imports tailwindcss then ../../tokens.css, so the DESIGN.md @theme is consumed as both var(--…) and utilities. src/App.tsx renders palette/status/gradients/type scale/spacing/radius/shadows entirely from tokens (no literals). dev (port 5181), build (tsc -b && vite build), typecheck, lint all pass. Tokens verified present in built CSS. Browser-verified via Playwright/Chrome screenshot — all sections render correctly (display headlines fall back to system sans until fonts land in US-003). Commands documented in design/README.md + AGENTS.md Unknown Stack.

userStories.title: Install brand fonts (web loaded; RN assets + config delivered)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the brand fonts available so type renders as designed — applied on web now, with RN assets and registration config ready for PRD-03 to bundle.
acceptanceCriteria: Space Grotesk (display) and Inter (text) self-hosted (or license-compliant source) and applied via the font tokens in the showcase harness.
Font files committed under design/fonts/ with an expo-font registration snippet/config documented for PRD-03 to bundle (RN runtime registration verified later in PRD-03).
Web and RN fallback stacks defined so missing glyphs degrade gracefully.
A sample heading renders in Space Grotesk and body text in Inter in the browser.
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill
verification: Load the harness; confirm the heading and body render in the correct faces; capture a screenshot.
Confirm design/fonts/ contains the font files plus a documented expo-font config.
userStories.notes: Self-hosted Space Grotesk (Google Fonts/SIL OFL) + Inter v4.1 (rsms/inter, OFL) under design/fonts/ (inter/ + space-grotesk/ + licenses). Web: design/fonts.css declares one variable @font-face per family (full 100-900 axis, font-display swap), imported after tokens.css in showcase src/index.css; vite.config.ts adds server.fs.allow:['..'] so the dev server serves design/fonts/. RN: design/fonts/expo-font.config.ts exports brandFontsVariable (preferred) + brandFontsStatic (portable) keyed to theme.font.* names, with variable TTFs + static Inter weight TTFs and the Space Grotesk variable TTF bundled for PRD-03. Fallback stacks live in --font-display/--font-sans (web) and system font (RN). Added a Fonts specimen section to App.tsx. typecheck+lint+build pass; Inter woff2 and Space Grotesk TTF emitted to dist. Browser-verified via Playwright/Chrome: document.fonts shows Inter + Space Grotesk 'loaded', check() true for both; H1/display render in Space Grotesk, body in Inter. Docs: design/README.md Brand fonts section + AGENTS.md Unknown Stack.

userStories.title: Core control components from tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I want buttons, chips, and badges built from tokens so every screen reuses them.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary filled button (black/white), ghost button, filter chip, and pill badge implemented per DESIGN.md specs in the showcase harness.
All states present: default/hover/pressed/disabled, plus loading for the primary button.
Semantic status variants (Done/Processing/Failed) wired to the status tokens on the badge.
Components reference tokens only — no literal hex/px/font values in component code.
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill
verification: Grep component source for literal hex/px/font values — none found.
Load the harness and exercise each component state.
userStories.notes: Built Button (primary + ghost), Chip/ChipGroup, and Badge under design/showcase/src/components/, styled 100% from tokens via src/components.css (real :hover/:active/:disabled pseudo-states + .is-force-* classes so the showcase renders states statically). Primary: black/white fill, on-photo white invert, hover(0.85)/pressed(0.7)/disabled(silver/fog)/loading(spinner, holds size). Ghost: black hairline → fills black on hover/pressed → silver/fog disabled. Filter chip: tint-soft pill container, active = white pill + --shadow-sm, on-photo active label = Warm Sand. Badge: soft/strong ink-tint pills + semantic status dot (Done/Processing/Failed) read from --color-status-* tokens. Added 4 missing tokens to DESIGN.md + tokens.css + theme.ts first: --text-label(12px)/--text-ui-lg(16px) + leadings, --surface-tint-soft/-strong (rgba ink washes). Grep: no hex/font/font-size literals in component code (only 1px/2px border & focus-outline widths, matching the existing US-002 pattern). typecheck+lint+build pass; new tokens + component classes present in built CSS. Browser-verified via Playwright/Chrome: computed styles resolve to the right tokens (primary #000/#fff r8, disabled #ccc, ghost 16px, active-chip white+shadow-sm, on-photo active #d9c58b, badge 12px tint-soft, status dots #31c431/#ffae00/#ff0000) — screenshot archive/2026-06-30-initial-scaffold/showcase-us004-core-controls-section.png.

userStories.title: Feed photo tile & before/after card
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the hero photography components since they anchor both the app feed and the marketing gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Feed photo tile: ~3:4 portrait, 12px radius, full-bleed image, optional category chip + lock/Premium badge over a legibility scrim, shimmer loading state.
Before/after card built from two tiles (or a slider) with Before/After pill labels.
Both built from tokens; pressed/selected states defined.
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill
verification: Load the harness; confirm aspect ratio, radius, scrim, shimmer, and Before/After labels.
userStories.notes: Built FeedPhotoTile (the hero component) + BeforeAfterCard under design/showcase/src/components/, styled 100% from tokens in src/components.css. Tile:

userStories.title: Content card & modal/sheet surfaces
userStories.description: As a developer, I want card and overlay surfaces so cards and modals are on-brand.
acceptanceCriteria: Standard content card (white, 12px radius, --shadow-sm, max two type sizes) implemented.
Modal/sheet surface (16px radius, --shadow-lg) implemented — derived from the --radius-modals token and the paywall surface spec.
Built from tokens; no literal visual values.
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill
verification: Load the harness; confirm card radius/shadow, the two-type-size limit, and the modal radius/shadow.
userStories.notes: Built Card (content card) + Sheet (modal/sheet surface) under design/showcase/src/components/, styled 100% from tokens in src/components.css. Card: white --surface-card, --radius-cards(12px), --shadow-sm, padding 12/16/16; exactly two type sizes — title --text-heading-sm(18px) bold ink + body --text-body(14px) regular slate; optional `media` slot bleeds a FeedPhotoTile flush to the padded top edge. Sheet: one white surface (--surface-card, --shadow-lg) in two variants — modal (--radius-modals 16px, centered) and sheet (16px top corners only + grab handle, bottom-anchored). Two modes: `inline` renders just the panel for the static showcase; overlay mode renders a fixed --surface-tint-strong scrim + role=dialog panel dismissed by scrim-click / Escape / close (×) button. No new tokens needed (radius-modals, shadow-lg, surface-card/tint-strong all existed). Modal max-width = calc(--spacing-160 * 3) ≈480px (unitless multiplier, like US-005's BA width). typecheck+lint+build pass; literal grep over Card/Sheet/components.css clean. Browser-verified via Playwright/Chrome: card radius 12px / shadow-sm / white / title 18px / body 14px rgb(102,102,102); modal radius 16px / shadow-lg / white; sheet top-radius 16px bottom 0 / shadow-lg; overlay scrim rgba(0,0,0,.2)=tint-strong, Escape + scrim-click both dismiss. Screenshots: archive/2026-06-30-initial-scaffold/showcase-us006-cards-surfaces-section.png + showcase-us006-modal-overlay.png + showcase-us006-sheet-overlay.png.

userStories.title: Paywall surface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an on-brand paywall surface so the subscription/credits flow matches the system.
acceptanceCriteria: Paywall surface per spec: selectable plan cards (selected = black border / black fill accent), price prominent in display type, 'Restore purchases' link present, black primary CTA.
Gradient wash allowed as the section background only — never on the button or other interactive elements.
Built from tokens; no literal visual values.
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill
verification: Load the harness; confirm plan-selection state, price prominence, restore link, black CTA, and that no gradient appears on the button.
userStories.notes: Built Paywall under design/showcase/src/components/Paywall.tsx, composed ON TOP of the US-006 Sheet (reuses its overlay/scrim/16px-radius/--shadow-lg surface, Escape/close dismiss) — added the selection body + footer only. Plan cards (

userStories.title: Reference showcase page (assemble & verify the whole system)
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want one page rendering the whole system so I can verify the intended look and catch drift.
acceptanceCriteria: The showcase harness renders the full system: palette, gradients, type scale, spacing scale, and every component variant/state from US-004 through US-007.
Everything consumes the exported tokens from design/ (proving the pipeline), not hardcoded copies.
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using the agent-browser skill; capture a screenshot in progress.txt
verification: Load the showcase; confirm every section is present and token-driven; save a screenshot to progress.txt.
userStories.notes: Assembled the full system into one navigable reference page in design/showcase/src/App.tsx. Added a token-only in-page Nav (table of contents, grouped Foundations / Components, 12 anchor links → section ids, bg --surface-tint-soft) and a GroupHeading divider component (Space Grotesk display eyebrow+title). Restructured the page into Foundations (Fonts, Palette, Status colors, Gradients, Type scale, Spacing, Radius, Shadows) then Components (Core controls US-004, Feed & before/after US-005, Cards & surfaces US-006, Paywall US-007) — every US-004→US-007 variant/state already rendered; this story is the assembly + final verification pass. All sections carry scroll-target ids. No new tokens; no component changes; everything still consumes design/tokens.css (proving the pipeline). typecheck+lint+build pass (dist CSS 22.05kB). Browser-verified via Playwright/Chrome: all 12 nav anchors resolve (0 missing), both group headings present, 12 section[id]s present, nav bg rgba(0,0,0,0.05), h1 Space Grotesk, primary button rgb(0,0,0); anchor nav scrolls to #paywall. Screenshots: archive/2026-06-30-initial-scaffold/showcase-us008-full-reference.png (full page) + showcase-us008-components-anchor.png. [2026-07-01 scope update] The reference page now assembles Foundations + US-004/US-005/US-006 components (11 sections); the Paywall section/nav entry was removed per the US-007 scope decision (app-only surface, not shown on the website). Re-verified: 11 sections, no #paywall anchor, no horizontal overflow at desktop or 390px mobile.

userStories.title: Enforce the system in agent instructions
userStories.description: As the team, I want the design rule baked into the Ralph loop so the agent follows DESIGN.md every iteration.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md instruct every iteration to read DESIGN.md and forbid hardcoded visual values (tokens only).
progress.txt ## Codebase Patterns records: 'DESIGN.md is the visual source of truth; consume tokens, never hardcode color/type/spacing/radius.'
A reviewer checklist item exists in checklist.md to reject PRs that hardcode visual values or diverge from DESIGN.md.
Typecheck passes
verification: Grep CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md for the read-DESIGN.md / tokens-only rule.
Confirm the progress.txt pattern line and the checklist.md reviewer item exist.
userStories.notes: Baked the design rule into the Ralph loop. CLAUDE.md Design Requirements: explicit 'Every iteration, read DESIGN.md before any UI work' (non-negotiable, applies every loop not just the first), tokens-only forbids hardcoded color/type/spacing/radius/shadow with the concrete token paths (design/tokens.css web / design/theme.ts RN), and a 'Reject any change… token-only is a merge gate' line. AGENTS.md Operating Rules: same every-iteration read-DESIGN.md + tokens-only rule plus a Reviewer gate line; QA Gate now lists 'UI code is token-only — matches DESIGN.md'. progress.txt ## Codebase Patterns line records verbatim: 'DESIGN.md is the visual source of truth; consume tokens, never hardcode color/type/spacing/radius.' checklist.md adds a US-009 section with a Standing reviewer gate (reject PRs that hardcode a visual value OR diverge from DESIGN.md, confirm DESIGN.md was read, token-only grep) + story-acceptance items. Verified via grep across all four files; typecheck green (tokens tsc + showcase tsc -b --noEmit). Docs-only story — no UI, no browser evidence. This completes PRD-01.
